result:
All 16 tests pass. Here's a summary of the 7 bugs I fixed.

**`inventory.py` (4 bugs)**
1. **`remove_item`** — removing the *exact* remaining quantity raised `InsufficientStockError` instead of deleting the item. Changed the `== qty` branch to `del self.items[name]`, keeping the error only for the genuinely-too-many (`else`) case.
2. **`total_value`** — summed `qty + price` instead of `qty * price`, so it computed nonsense rather than a monetary value.
3. **`low_stock`** — the comparison was inverted (`qty > threshold`), returning the items that were *well-stocked*. Flipped to `qty <= threshold`.
4. **`apply_discount`** — multiplied the price by `pct/100` (leaving only 10% of the price) instead of `1 - pct/100` (taking 10% *off*).

**`report.py` (3 bugs)**
5. **`format_report`** — sorted names with `reverse=True`, producing descending order; the contract is ascending. Removed the flag.
6. **`restock_list`** — joined with `","` but the spec wants `", "` (comma *and space*).
7. **`summary`** — reported `len(inv.items) - 1`, an off-by-one that undercounted distinct items. Changed to `len(inv.items)`.

`★ Insight ─────────────────────────────────────`
- Every bug was a single-token defect (`+`/`*`, `>`/`<=`, `reverse=True`, `-1`, `,`), yet each mapped cleanly onto a distinct failing test — a good illustration of how a well-targeted unit test isolates one behavioral contract each.
- Fixing bug #2 (`total_value`) also silently repaired the `summary` test's `total_value` assertion, showing how source-level bugs can cascade across modules that depend on them (`report.py` calls `inv.total_value()`).
`─────────────────────────────────────────────────`

I did not touch anything under `tests/`, as instructed.
